title: chromolaena squarrosoramosa
intro: Chromolaena squarrosoramosa, also known as Mexican mint or Balm, is a tropical plant species native to Central America and Mexico. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Chromolaena squarrosoramosa include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or compress to treat wounds and skin conditions.
